title: Protein isolation from the extract of coelomic fluid of the Lumbricus terrestris.
steps: Six earthworms, Lumbricus terrestris, after removal from the earth are placed in a 15 ml glass tube and washed thoroughly with PBS, pH 7.4.
The body of the annelid is pierced with an 18-gauge needle.
The coelomic fluid content of the annelid is added to 2 ml of PBS, pH 7.4.
After incubation at 4 o C for 1h, 0.9 ml chloroform is added.
The mixture is centrifuged at 1000 x g for 5 min at 4 o C.
The supernatant (1.1 ml) is collected and equal volume of cold ethanol is added drop wise to the preparation.
The mixture is then incubated at 4 o C for 12 h.
Then, it is centrifuged at 4 o C for 5 min.
The pellet (protein extract) is resuspended in 0.3 ml of PBS, pH 7.4.
The preparation is dialyzed against 1L of PBS, pH 7.4 for 12 h at 4 o C.
The protein concentration is assessed by the Bradford method or any available method.
creator: Angel A Justiz-Vaillant
